svg.text: A fractal of infinite complexity from a simple rule

text.tspan: Every smaller triangle is an exact
copy of the whole shape. Zoom in
Start with one triangle. Remove the
centre to get three smaller copies.
Repeat on each copy — that’s recursion!
A line is 1-D. A square is 2-D.
quite a line, not quite a plane,
Color the odd entries of Pascal’s
Pick any starting point inside the
The dots trace the Sierpiński triangle!
At each step, ¼ of remaining area
steps the total area reaches zero —

svg.text: Wacław Sierpiński first described this fractal in 1915.
